pagesAudited.title: Home
pagesAudited.role: Landing page (hero, cards, stats, quote, CTA)

pagesAudited.title: My Practice (Beyond Pets Animal Hospital)
pagesAudited.role: Service detail page (hero, about, services, training, CTA)

toolInventory.bricksMCP: NOT CONNECTED - zero Bricks/WordPress MCP tools available
toolInventory.playwrightMCP: Full browser automation (navigate, screenshot, evaluate, snapshot, network, console)

details.note: Skip links are only visible on focus. Low priority but technically fails WCAG AA.

Programmatic contrast check returns 1:1 because it cannot see through CSS background-image overlays. Visual inspection shows white text on dark blue overlay over the clinic photo. Estimated contrast ratio is 7:1+ based on overlay darkness. PASSES AA visually but needs manual verification.

details.element: Primary CTA buttons (Learn My Story, Get in Touch)

issues: Footer H3 ('Dr. Phil Good, DVM') should arguably be H2 for consistency, but H3 in footer is acceptable

practicePageOutline: H2: A Practice Built on Family
practiceIssues: Heading hierarchy is clean and logical

homePage.headline: Dr. Phil Good, DVM
homePage.description: Leading Beyond Pets Animal Hospital in Marietta, Georgia since 1979...
weaknesses: Background image (old clinic building sign) feels dated and not aspirational
Hero is very tall (100vh) - CTAs pushed below the fold on smaller desktop screens
Subtitle text at top is small relative to hero height
No professional photo of Dr. Good himself - missed personal branding opportunity

practicePage.headline: Beyond Pets Animal Hospital
practicePage.subheadline: Formerly Town & Country Veterinary Hospital · Serving Marietta Since 1979
weaknesses: Same background image as home page - feels repetitive
No CTA buttons in hero
Hero has no interactive elements to encourage scroll

strengths: Body font 18px with 27px line height (1.5 ratio) - excellent readability
Max content width 1200px centered - good reading measure
Cards have consistent 12px border-radius and standardized shadows
weaknesses: Home page cards stack vertically on desktop (single column) instead of 3-column grid - wastes horizontal space at 1440px
Large gaps between cards on desktop due to vertical stacking
Stats section (45+, 20000+, 1) is quite compact - could have more visual weight
Quote section has generous padding but the opening quote mark renders as '201C' unicode character instead of styled quotation mark

strengths: Clear heading hierarchy with consistent sizing
Good body text size for readability
weaknesses: Nav link size (15px) is slightly small for a 1440px desktop viewport
Card 'Learn More' links at 14px are small for touch targets

details.usage: Hero background on both Home and My Practice pages
details.assessment: Shows old 'Town & Country Veterinary Clinic' signage with cars in parking lot. Not aspirational. Reused across pages. No WebP/AVIF format detected.
details.recommendation: Replace with professional photo. Use different images per page. Serve in modern formats.

details.assessment: Logo PNG - massively oversized for display (848px served for 141px display). No srcset or responsive sizing.
details.recommendation: Serve appropriately sized logo. Consider SVG for better scaling.

details.usage: About section image on My Practice page
details.assessment: Reasonable sizing but could use srcset for responsive delivery

vsTargets.LCP: ~1.7s (estimated from FCP, well under 2.5s target - PASSES)
vsTargets.INP: Not measurable via single-page audit (would need real user interaction)
vsTargets.CLS: No visible layout shifts observed during audit

strengths: Zero horizontal overflow on all viewports
weaknesses: 15+ third-party scripts loaded (GSAP, ScrollTrigger, ScrollSmoother, Swup, New Relic, Google Analytics, BricksForge, BricksExtras)
Swup page transition library loaded regardless of whether transitions are used
New Relic SPA agent is heavy for a brochure site
Multiple GSAP/Swup sub-libraries loaded individually rather than bundled
Logo PNG (848x240) served for 141x40 display
No preload hints visible for hero background image

breakpointBehavior.navigation: Desktop: horizontal nav links (15px). Tablet/Mobile: hamburger menu. Transition works cleanly.
breakpointBehavior.heroSection: Scales well across breakpoints. Text remains centered and readable.
breakpointBehavior.cards: Stack vertically on all viewports (already single-column on desktop, so no change needed for smaller screens)
breakpointBehavior.statsSection: Desktop: 3 columns. Tablet: appears to wrap. Mobile: stacks vertically. Clean transitions.
breakpointBehavior.footer: Desktop: 4 columns. Tablet: wraps to 2x2 or stacks. Mobile: single column.
breakpointBehavior.ctaSection: Consistent across breakpoints. Button remains centered.

strengths: No horizontal overflow on any viewport
Hamburger menu properly appears at tablet breakpoint
Footer adapts well to all viewports
CTAs remain accessible and tappable on mobile
weaknesses: Cards are already single-column on desktop - not leveraging wider viewports
My Practice mobile hero text could be slightly larger for readability

scrollAnimations.description: Scroll-triggered reveal animations (opacity: 0 -> 1 with translateY) are applied to major content sections via the 'reveal' CSS class
strengths: Smooth, professional animation feel when scrolling
weaknesses: Content is completely invisible (opacity: 0) until scrolled into view
Full-page screenshots capture sections at opacity: 0 - appears as blank white page to automated tools
CSS !important overrides cannot reliably fix this because IntersectionObserver re-applies styles
Search engine crawlers may not execute JS animations, potentially affecting perceived content
Reduced-motion preference should disable these animations (not verified)
If JS fails to load, content remains invisible

bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.symptom: No Bricks MCP tools available in the current Claude Code session
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.rootCause: Bricks MCP server not connected/configured as an MCP server for this session
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.proposedImprovement: Ensure Bricks MCP plugin is registered as an MCP server in Claude Code's MCP config. Provide clear setup docs for connecting the WordPress REST API endpoint.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.expectedImpact: Without this, zero page creation or modification is possible. This blocks the entire automated page-building workflow.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.idealFutureBehavior: Bricks MCP tools appear alongside Playwright tools automatically when the WordPress site URL is configured.

bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.symptom: Full-page screenshots show blank white areas where content exists but has opacity: 0 due to scroll-reveal animations
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.rootCause: Reveal animations use IntersectionObserver to toggle opacity. Playwright's full-page screenshot scrolls and stitches without triggering the observer, or the observer resets when viewport moves.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.proposedImprovement: Bricks MCP should provide a 'disable-animations' mode for audit/screenshot purposes, or a render tool that forces all elements visible before capture.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.expectedImpact: Would make automated visual QA possible without manual scroll-and-capture workarounds.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.idealFutureBehavior: bricks/render-page --disable-animations captures the full page with all elements visible.

bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.symptom: Hero background image reused across pages; logo PNG massively oversized (848px for 141px display); no WebP/AVIF; no srcset
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.rootCause: No built-in image optimization or responsive image generation in the page creation workflow
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.proposedImprovement: Auto-generate responsive srcset, serve WebP/AVIF, warn when image natural size > 2x display size, encourage unique images per page.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.expectedImpact: Better LCP scores, reduced bandwidth, more professional per-page visual identity.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.idealFutureBehavior: When assigning an image, auto-optimize and add srcset. Warn if same hero image used across multiple pages.

bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.symptom: Home page cards render as single-column on desktop despite 1200px content width available for a 3-column grid
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.rootCause: Cards use a vertically stacked layout that doesn't adapt to wider viewports
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.proposedImprovement: Page blueprints should default to responsive grid layouts (3-col desktop, 2-col tablet, 1-col mobile) for card-type content.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.expectedImpact: Better use of horizontal space on desktop, more professional appearance.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.idealFutureBehavior: bricks/generate-blueprint automatically creates responsive grid layouts for card patterns.

bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.symptom: Quote section renders '201C' unicode escape instead of styled quotation mark
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.rootCause: Raw unicode character inserted as text content instead of being rendered as a styled element
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.proposedImprovement: Template/blueprint for quote sections should use CSS ::before pseudo-element for decorative quotes, not raw text content.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.expectedImpact: Eliminates visual glitch in quote sections.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.idealFutureBehavior: Quote section templates include CSS-based decorative quotation marks.

bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.symptom: 15+ third-party scripts loaded (GSAP, ScrollSmoother, Swup, New Relic, GA, etc.) for a brochure site
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.rootCause: BricksForge loads full GSAP suite + Swup page transitions regardless of whether they're needed per-page
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.proposedImprovement: Script audit tool that identifies unused JS libraries per page. Conditional loading based on which elements actually use animations/transitions.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.expectedImpact: Faster TTI, smaller JS payload, better mobile performance.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.idealFutureBehavior: bricks/audit-performance warns about unused script libraries and suggests removal.

bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.symptom: Skip link contrast fails WCAG AA (2.67:1)
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.rootCause: Skip link styling uses light pink on medium red background
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.proposedImprovement: Default skip link styling should pass WCAG AA. Accessibility checks should be part of the generation pipeline.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.idealFutureBehavior: bricks/validate-accessibility checks skip links, focus states, and contrast before marking a page complete.

bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.symptom: Cards on home page use generic div containers rather than semantic elements like article or section with aria labels
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.rootCause: Bricks Builder generates div-based layouts by default
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.proposedImprovement: Templates should use semantic HTML (article for cards, section for page sections, nav for navigation lists). Admin labels should map to aria-label.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.expectedImpact: Better screen reader experience, improved SEO semantics.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.idealFutureBehavior: Card patterns automatically use <article> elements with admin labels as aria-labels.

bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.symptom: No example prompts or templates demonstrating 'ideal single-prompt page generation'
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.rootCause: Workflow documentation focuses on individual tools rather than end-to-end generation patterns
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.proposedImprovement: Provide example prompts that produce high-quality pages in one shot, with annotated before/after examples.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.expectedImpact: Dramatically reduces prompt iteration needed to build quality pages.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.idealFutureBehavior: Documentation includes 5-10 tested prompt templates for common page types (landing, service, about, contact, blog).

bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.symptom: No automated post-generation audit loop exists
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.rootCause: Page generation and visual validation are separate manual processes
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.proposedImprovement: After generating a page, automatically run Playwright audit (heading structure, contrast, overflow, CLS) and report issues before marking complete.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.expectedImpact: Would catch 80% of visual issues before user review.
bricksMCPWorkflowGaps.idealFutureBehavior: bricks/generate-page runs bricks/audit-page automatically after creation and returns both the page and an audit summary.
